title: Deprecate custom WSL user-mode networking in favor of Mirrored Mode
body:
### Feature request description

Currently, Podman Machine on WSL utilizes a custom user-mode networking implementation (relying on gvforwarder/gvproxy) to manage traffic. This involves creating a custom Podman machine, manipulating routing tables, and using userspace processes to handle traffic.

However, because of how WSL works, this implementation affects all WSL instances (even non-podman machine). All traffic is redirected to gvforwarder and this can occasionally result in routing conflicts.

Historically, this architecture was required to bypass VPN issues but Microsoft released [Mirrored mode](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/networking#mirrored-mode-networking) in Sep 2023 to solve this exact same problems.

### Suggest potential solution

I would propose that Podman begins to deprecate the custom gvforwarder implementation and start relying on the native WSL 2 Mirrored Mode networking to solve such connectivity issues.

The only constraint is that Mirrored Mode only works on Windows 11 22H2 or higher, but as podman 6 should only target Windows 11 it should be safe.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Due to the nature of WSL (one kernel shared among all instances) there is no other alternative i can think of

### Additional context

This issue came up when we started working on supporting RHEL machines (for the Podman Desktop RHEL extension) on WSL. If there is one user-mode podman machine running, all traffic is going through the same gvforwarder gateway. If we rely on Mirrored Mode we could remove from our shoulders the burden to maintain such solution and avoid routing conflicts.

comments.body: Mirrored networking is not available on all Windows SKUs, specifically server editions https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/11154
There were also various issues on this repo that mention podman machine doesn't work properly with mirrored mode, I see at least https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22975

I am running Podman successfully on 2 Windows machines (one wired, another wifi) with WSL in mirrored mode. With `generateResolvConf=true` and `dnsTunneling=true` which are defaults, everything seem to work fine.

However, when I try to bypass the DNS Tunnel in the WiFi system (e.g. `generateResolvConf=false` and a custom resolv.conf for the base machine), DNS resolver seems to break.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
